name: Calabacitas (Southwest)
recipeIngredient: 6 cups cubed green summer squash and/or yellow summer squash
1 red bell pepper, cored and seeded, diced
1/2 cup Anaheim chili pepper, roasted, cored and seeded, peeled and diced
1 teaspoon pico de gallo (preferably Don Enrique)
fresh ground black pepper to taste
1 cup lowfat shredded Monterey Jack cheese or queso fresco, crumbled
recipeInstructions:
1. Soften onion with garlic over medium heat in a sprayed skillet. Or, if using butter, melt it over medium heat in a large skillet and add the onion and garlic. Saute for 2 minutes.
2. Add the squash and bell pepper and cook another 2 minutes. Stir in the chiles, corn, and seasonings.
3. Cover, lower the heat, and simmer until the squash is tender, about 3-5 minutes.
4. Uncover, sprinkle with cheese and stir until cheese is melted. Serve immediately.
Notes: Information and recipe adapted from Southwest The Beautiful Cookbook; recipes by Barbara Pool Fenzl, text by Norman Kolpas, CollinsPublishers San Francisco, 1994. Recipes featured at Melissa's Specialty foods http://www.melissas.com Sept 1998: number of servings not listed; pat's guesses. Mastercook versions kitpath@earthlink.net Serves 4 small portions. (105 cals, 2g fat)
